pr_294.title: fix(post_evals): close numeric-id fail-open in smoke_run
pr_294.verifying_check: Self-executed (not agent-claimed): (1) `bash hooks/scripts/tests/post_evals.test.sh` run in this session, tail output 'PASS'; (2) numeric id (`{"id":1,...}`) piped to `./scripts/post_evals.sh smoke-run` exited 1 with stderr 'a scripted eval has a non-string id (schema requires id to be a string) — refusing.'; (3) string id (`{"id":"1",...}`) piped to the same command exited 0 and wrote real observed evidence `{"cmd_exit":0,"negative_control_exit":1,...}` to the eval's .smoke field, confirming the honest path still executes and records genuine evidence rather than being merely unblocked.
pr_294.self_mergeable_reason: Neither changed file (scripts/post_evals.sh, hooks/scripts/tests/post_evals.test.sh) matches TIER_GATE_PATH_DENYLIST, unlike PR #288 and PR #292 in this same session which required the denylist route.

hook_blocks: no-record: dc_mine_hook_blocks mines a SESSION's discipline log against a completed loop's session_id; this session ran no agentic loop, so mining it here would misrepresent an ordinary documentation/verification session as loop activity. Not run.
review_themes: This session handled three defects sequentially: PR #288 (notification leak in tests), PR #292 (U1 terminal marker), and PR #294 (this one). PR #294's defect class matches a pattern already recorded in standing-orders.md: a gate's evidence-gathering step (smoke_run) could return empty without erroring, and empty was silently treated as 'ran, nothing to report' rather than as a refusal — the same shape as SO-58 (empty artifact body satisfies the gate) and SO-65 (a malformed eval structure passing its own merge gate), applied here to a jq type-mismatch rather than an authoring or embedding defect.
raw_notes: PROVENANCE: the PR #294 defect was found incidentally, not via a dedicated audit — an eval-authoring worker building artifacts for a different PR in this session hit the null-smoke symptom (`smoke: {cmd_exit: null, ...}` recorded for an eval that should have run), and the mechanism (jq `--arg` always binds a string, so `select(.id == $id)` never matches a JSON-number id) was then verified from source. SIBLING FACT: the same file's check 10 (`post_evals::validate_smoke_execution`, scripts/post_evals.sh:415,423-424) is NOT vulnerable to this defect, but for a reason unrelated to correctness — it iterates `.evals[$idx]` by array INDEX (`--argjson i "$idx"`), not via the `select(.id == $id)` string-bound lookup pattern used at lines 258, 272-273, 719-720 (now guarded), 743, 1043, 1050-1053. The commit message for 35dabcc notes validate_smoke and validate_discriminating share the same `select(.id == $id)` pattern as the fixed smoke_run but were left unchanged because their existing shape checks already fail closed on a numeric id, just with a less clear message — i.e. those sites are already safe, only smoke_run had the fail-open (silent empty → exit 0) consequence.
